faqs.question: How quickly can you buy my house in Ealing?
faqs.answer: We can complete the purchase of your Ealing property in as little as seven days from the point you accept our offer. Our average completion time across the London Borough of Ealing is 14 days. Because we buy with our own cash funds and there is no chain, there are no delays caused by mortgage approvals, lender surveys, or chain dependencies. You choose the completion date that works best for your circumstances, whether that is one week or several weeks from now.

faqs.question: How much will you offer for my Ealing property?
faqs.answer: Our offers are based on a detailed analysis of recent comparable sales in your specific Ealing neighbourhood — whether that is Ealing Broadway, Acton, Hanwell, Southall, Northolt, Greenford, or Perivale. We typically offer between 80% and 90% of the full open market value, reflecting the speed, certainty, and convenience we provide. When you factor in estate agent fees, months of holding costs, and the risk of chain collapse, many sellers find the net financial outcome is comparable to a traditional sale.

faqs.question: Are there any fees or hidden costs?
faqs.answer: No. When you sell your Ealing property to us, the price we offer is the amount you receive. We do not charge estate agent commissions, administration fees, valuation charges, or any other costs. We also cover all legal fees associated with the transaction, instructing experienced conveyancers who specialise in fast completions. There are no deductions at any stage of the process.

faqs.question: Do you buy properties in any condition?
faqs.answer: Yes. We buy houses and flats in any condition across the London Borough of Ealing. Whether your property is in excellent order, needs cosmetic updating, or requires major structural work, we will make you a fair cash offer. We regularly purchase properties with damp issues, subsidence, outdated interiors, non-standard construction, and other problems that would deter traditional buyers or prevent mortgage lending. You do not need to spend money on repairs before selling to us.

faqs.question: Do I need to have my property valued before contacting you?
faqs.answer: No. We carry out our own free, no-obligation valuation using detailed local market data for the Ealing area. Our team analyses recent Land Registry sales, current market conditions, and property-specific factors to arrive at a fair offer. If you already have a valuation from an estate agent or surveyor, we are happy to discuss how our offer compares and explain the reasoning behind our pricing.

faqs.question: Can you buy my property if I still have a mortgage?
faqs.answer: Absolutely. The majority of properties we purchase in Ealing have an outstanding mortgage. On completion, your solicitor uses the sale proceeds to repay the mortgage in full, and any remaining equity is transferred directly to you. We work with your mortgage lender and solicitor to ensure this process is seamless. If your mortgage balance is close to or exceeds the property value, we can discuss your options openly and advise on the best course of action.

faqs.question: What areas of Ealing do you cover?
faqs.answer: We buy properties across the entire London Borough of Ealing, including Ealing Broadway, Acton, Hanwell, Southall, Northolt, Greenford, Perivale, West Ealing, Pitshanger, Drayton Green, and Dormer's Wells. We cover the W5 postcode and the wider borough postcodes including W3, W7, W13, UB1, UB2, UB5, and UB6. If your property is in the Ealing borough, we can help. Contact us on 0800 012 1007.

faqs.question: Will I need to pay for a solicitor?
faqs.answer: No. We cover all legal fees for the transaction, instructing experienced conveyancers who specialise in fast property completions. You are also welcome to instruct your own independent solicitor if you prefer, and we will contribute towards their costs. We always recommend that sellers have independent legal representation to ensure they are fully protected throughout the process.

faqs.question: Can you buy my house if it is tenanted?
faqs.answer: Yes. We regularly purchase tenanted properties across the London Borough of Ealing. This is particularly useful for landlords who want to exit the buy-to-let market without the disruption and cost of gaining vacant possession. We can buy your property with sitting tenants on assured shorthold tenancies, and we handle the transition professionally. This allows you to sell quickly while your tenants retain their home.

faqs.question: How is your service different from an estate agent?
faqs.answer: An estate agent markets your property to find a buyer, which typically takes five to seven months in Ealing and comes with no guarantee of completion. We are the buyer — we purchase your property directly with our own cash. This means no marketing period, no viewings, no chain, and no risk of the sale falling through. We charge no fees and cover your legal costs. Our offer may be below full open market value, but for many sellers the speed, certainty, and cost savings make it the better overall financial outcome.

faqs.question: What if I change my mind after accepting your offer?
faqs.answer: You are under no obligation until contracts are exchanged. If you accept our offer but your circumstances change before exchange, you are free to withdraw without penalty or cost. We understand that selling a property is a significant decision, and we never pressure homeowners into proceeding. Our goal is to provide a service you choose because it genuinely works for you, not because you feel committed.

faqs.question: Do you buy leasehold properties in Ealing?
faqs.answer: Yes. We buy both freehold and leasehold properties across the London Borough of Ealing. We have experience with a wide range of leasehold situations, including short leases, high service charges, ground rent disputes, buildings with cladding or fire safety concerns, and flats where the freeholder is unresponsive. If your leasehold property has complications that make it difficult to sell traditionally, a cash sale to us may be the most practical route.

faqs.question: Will you visit my property before making an offer?
faqs.answer: We can often make an initial cash offer based on the information you provide and our desktop analysis of the Ealing market. For most properties, we will arrange a brief in-person visit before confirming our final offer. This visit typically takes around 30 minutes and allows us to verify the condition and features of your property so our offer is as accurate as possible. We schedule visits at a time that suits you.

faqs.question: Can you help if I am facing repossession in Ealing?
faqs.answer: Yes. If your mortgage lender has begun repossession proceedings on your Ealing property, acting quickly is essential. A voluntary sale through a cash buyer will almost always achieve a better outcome than a forced sale by the lender. You retain more control, better protect your credit rating, and typically receive a higher price. If you are in this situation, contact us immediately on 0800 012 1007 so we can assess your options and move swiftly.

faqs.question: How do I know your offer is fair?
faqs.answer: We base our offers on verifiable market data, including recent Land Registry sales of comparable properties in your specific Ealing neighbourhood. We share this data with you so you can see exactly how we arrived at our figure. We encourage you to obtain independent valuations and to consult a financial adviser or solicitor before accepting. Our confidence in the fairness of our offers means we welcome scrutiny — transparency is fundamental to how we operate.
